---
title: "Unidad 10.2: Reverse Proxy y Balanceo de Carga"
subtitle: "Nginx como gateway para aplicaciones backend"
author: "Diego Saavedra"
date: "2026-03-31"
date-format: medium
---

# Unidad 10.2: Reverse Proxy y Balanceo de Carga

## Introducción

Un reverse proxy es la puerta de entrada estándar en arquitecturas modernas. Nginx recibe las solicitudes y las distribuye a los backend, permitiendo SSL termination, caching, logging centralizado, y balanceo de carga.

## Concepto de Reverse Proxy

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                 REVERSE PROXY EN ACCIÓN                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   CLIENTE                 NGINX                   BACKENDS     │
│  ┌────────┐            ┌──────────┐           ┌──────────┐    │
│  │        │  HTTPS :443 │          │  HTTP :3000│ Node.js  │    │
│  │ Navegador│ ────────> │ Nginx    │ ─────────> │ App      │    │
│  │         │            │ (proxy)  │           │          │    │
│  └────────┘            └──────────┘           └──────────┘    │
│                                                                  │
│   + SSL Termination          + Logs centralizados               │
│   + Load Balancing            + Cache de respuestas              │
│   + WAF básico               + Alta disponibilidad             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Configuración de Reverse Proxy

### Proxy Básico

```bash
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/sites-available/backend-proxy.conf <<'EOF'
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name api.example.com;

    location / {
        # Proxy al backend
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
        
        # Headers esenciales
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
        
        # Timeouts
        proxy_connect_timeout 60s;
        proxy_send_timeout 60s;
        proxy_read_timeout 60s;
    }
}
EOF

$ sudo ln -sf /etc/nginx/sites-available/backend-proxy.conf /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
$ sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx
# <1>
```

1. **Proxy completo**: Headers, timeouts, reescritura de headers

### Verificar Funcionamiento

```bash
# Backend corriendo
$ curl http://127.0.0.1:3000
{"status": "ok", "version": "1.0.0"}

# A través de Nginx
$ curl -H 'Host: api.example.com' http://127.0.0.1
{"status": "ok", "version": "1.0.0"}

# Ver headers
$ curl -I -H 'Host: api.example.com' http://127.0.0.1 | grep -i proxy
X-Real-IP: 127.0.0.1
X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1
```

### Headers de Proxy Explicados

| Header | Descripción |
|--------|-------------|
| `Host` | Host original de la request |
| `X-Real-IP` | IP real del cliente |
| `X-Forwarded-For` | IP original (para proxys múltiples) |
| `X-Forwarded-Proto` | Protocolo original (http/https) |
| `X-Forwarded-Host` | Host original |

## Balanceo de Carga

### Upstream Basic

```bash
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/sites-available/loadbalancer.conf <<'EOF'
# Definir upstream (backend servers)
upstream api_backend {
    # Round robin (default)
    server 10.0.1.11:3000;
    server 10.0.1.12:3000;
    server 10.0.1.13:3000;
}

server {
    listen 80;
    server_name api.example.com;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://api_backend;
        
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    }
}
EOF
# <1>
```

1. **Upstream**: Define grupo de backends

### Algoritmos de Balanceo

```bash
# IP Hash (sticky sessions)
upstream api_backend {
    ip_hash;
    server 10.0.1.11:3000;
    server 10.0.1.12:3000;
    server 10.0.1.13:3000;
}

# Least Connections
upstream api_backend {
    least_conn;
    server 10.0.1.11:3000;
    server 10.0.1.12:3000;
    server 10.0.1.13:3000;
}

# Weighted (capacidad diferente)
upstream api_backend {
    server 10.0.1.11:3000 weight=5;  # 50%
    server 10.0.1.12:3000 weight=3;  # 30%
    server 10.0.1.13:3000 weight=2;  # 20%
}
```

### Health Checks y Backup

```bash
upstream api_backend {
    server 10.0.1.11:3000 max_fails=3 fail_timeout=30s;
    server 10.0.1.12:3000 max_fails=3 fail_timeout=30s;
    server 10.0.1.13:3000 backup;  # Solo si otros fallan
    server 10.0.1.14:3000 down;   # Deshabilitado
}
```

- **max_fails**: Intentos fallidos antes de marcar down
- **fail_timeout**: Tiempo antes de reintentar
- **backup**: Usa solo si otros fallan
- **down**: Deshabilitado temporalmente

## Proxy a Different Backends

### Node.js

```bash
location / {
    proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
    proxy_http_version 1.1;
    proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
    proxy_set_header Connection "upgrade";
    proxy_set_header Host $host;
}
```

### PHP-FPM

```bash
location ~ \.php$ {
    include snippets/fastcgi-php.conf;
    fastcgi_pass unix:/run/php/php8.1-fpm.sock;
    fastcgi_param SCRIPT_FILENAME $document_root$fastcgi_script_name;
}
```

### Python (Gunicorn/uvicorn)

```bash
location / {
    proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:8000;
    proxy_set_header Host $host;
    proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    
    # WebSocket support
    proxy_http_version 1.1;
    proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
    proxy_set_header Connection "upgrade";
}
```

### Docker Container

```bash
location / {
    proxy_pass http://172.17.0.2:8080;
    proxy_set_header Host $host;
    proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
}
```

## WebSocket Proxy

### Configuración WebSocket

```bash
location /ws/ {
    proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
    
    # WebSocket headers
    proxy_http_version 1.1;
    proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
    proxy_set_header Connection "upgrade";
    
    # Timeouts extendidos para long-lived connections
    proxy_read_timeout 3600s;
    proxy_send_timeout 3600s;
    
    # Buffers
    proxy_buffering off;
    proxy_request_buffering off;
}
```

### Verificar WebSocket

```bash
# Test con curl (handshake)
$ curl -i -N \
    -H "Connection: Upgrade" \
    -H "Upgrade: websocket" \
    -H "Sec-WebSocket-Version: 13" \
    -H "Sec-WebSocket-Key: test123==" \
    http://localhost/ws/
```

## Caching con Proxy

### Basic Proxy Cache

```bash
# En http block
proxy_cache_path /var/cache/nginx levels=1:2 keys_zone=api_cache:10M max_size=100M inactive=60m;

server {
    location /api/ {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
        proxy_cache api_cache;
        
        # Cache respuestas 200 por 60 min
        proxy_cache_valid 200 60m;
        
        # Keys diferentes por Host
        proxy_cache_key $scheme$host$request_uri;
        
        # Agregar header de cache status
        add_header X-Cache-Status $upstream_cache_status;
    }
}
```

### Cache con Purge

```bash
# Location para purgar cache
location ~ /purge(/.*) {
    proxy_cache_purge api_cache $scheme$host$1;
    access_log off;
}

# Usar: curl -X PURGE https://example.com/purge/api/data
```

## SSL Termination

### HTTPS a HTTP Backend

```bash
server {
    listen 443 ssl http2;
    server_name api.example.com;
    
    # SSL certs
    ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/fullchain.pem;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/privkey.pem;
    include /etc/letsencrypt/options-ssl-nginx.conf;
    
    # Proxy a backend HTTP
    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto https;
    }
}
```

## Casos de Estudio

### Caso 1: API con Múltiples Versiones

**Escenario**: API v1 y v2 corriendo en puertos diferentes.

```bash
# API v1
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name api.example.com;
    
    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
    }
}

# API v2 (nueva)
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name api-v2.example.com;
    
    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:4000;
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
    }
}
```

### Caso 2: Redirect según Path

**Escenario**: Diferentes backends según path URL.

```bash
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name example.com;
    
    # Frontend estático
    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:8080;
    }
    
    # API
    location /api/ {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
    }
    
    # Admin panel
    location /admin/ {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:5000;
        # Solo desde red local
        allow 10.0.0.0/8;
        deny all;
    }
}
```

### Caso 3: Blue-Green Deployment

**Escenario**: Cambiar tráfico gradualmente entre versiones.

```bash
# Blue (versión actual)
upstream blue {
    server 10.0.1.10:3000;
}

# Green (nueva versión)
upstream green {
    server 10.0.1.20:3000;
}

# Por defecto blue
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name example.com;
    
    location / {
        proxy_pass http://blue;
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
    }
}
```

## Resumen

| Tema | Comando Clave | Propósito |
|------|---------------|-----------|
| Proxy | `proxy_pass http://ip:port` | Forward request |
| Headers | `proxy_set_header` | Pass headers al backend |
| Upstream | `upstream name {}` | Grupo de backends |
| Balanceo | `ip_hash` / `least_conn` | Algoritmo de distribución |
| WebSocket | `Upgrade $http_upgrade` | WebSocket support |
| Cache | `proxy_cache` | Cachear respuestas |

## Referencias

- [Nginx Reverse Proxy](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx/admin-guide/web-server/reverse-proxy/)
- [Nginx Load Balancing](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx/admin-guide/load-balancer/http-load-balancer/)
- [Nginx Proxy Module](https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_proxy_module.html)
